content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the documentation structure. Let me create a detailed report:

## Summary Report: Kinsward Documentation Site Structure

### Full Sidebar Structure (from astro.config.mjs)

The documentation is organized into **4 main sections**:

1. **Getting Started** (3 pages)
   - Download
   - Installation
   - Controls

2. **Gameplay** (5 pages)
   - Adventure Mode
   - Overseer Mode
   - World Generation
   - Units & Companions
   - Crafting

3. **kinswardctl** (3 pages)
   - Overview
   - Commands Reference
   - Examples

4. **Development** (3 pages)
   - Roadmap
   - Changelog
   - Architecture

**Total: 14 documentation pages**

---

### Current Version Status

**Latest documented version: v0.25.0** (per changelog.md)
- The index.mdx mentions v0.21.0 in the "Current State" section (outdated)

---

### Changelog Format & Style (v0.22.0–v0.25.0)

The changelog follows a consistent structure:

**Format:**
- **Version header** (e.g., `## v0.25.0 (Current)`)
- Bullet list of features with **bold category labels**
- Deep technical details (API changes, system names, configuration)
- Examples of new or changed systems

**Style characteristics:**
- Technical terminology (no fluff)
- Bold labels for feature categories: `**feature name**`
- Parenthetical details after colons
- Focus on "what changed" rather than user-facing descriptions
- References to implementation details (e.g., `SpawnCmd`, `CameraCmdQueue`, `UiHovered`)

**Example from v0.25.0:**
```
- **14 new GameCommand variants**: SwitchMode, SetToolMode, CameraMove, CameraZoom, SelectUnit, DeselectAll, QuerySelection, SetActiveZ, SetFloorCover, RemoveFloorCover, QueryLight, QueryInventory, QueryPerf
- **kinswardctl**: 12 new subcommands with human-readable formatting (switch-mode, set-tool-mode, camera-move, camera-zoom, select, deselect-all, selection, set-active-z, set-floor-cover, remove-floor-cover, light, perf)
```

**v0.24.0 example:**
```
- **DebugPickingPlugin**: behind `cfg(debug)`, toggle with F3; F11 prints HoverMap to stderr
- **bevy_dev_tools**: added under `debug` feature flag
```

---

### Pages Needing Updates for v0.26.x

Based on the features you mentioned (game phases, CLI subcommands, debug mode enhancements, loading screen), here are the pages that will need updates:

#### 1. **Controls** (`src/content/docs/getting-started/controls.mdx`)
- **Current state:** Only documents F5, F9 (save/load)
- **Needs update:** 
  - F3 debug key (DebugPickingPlugin toggle) — already mentioned in v0.24.0 changelog
  - F11 debug key (HoverMap stderr print) — already mentioned in v0.24.0 changelog
  - New v0.26.x debug keys (if any)
  - Clarify that debug controls require `--debug` flag

#### 2. **kinswardctl/Commands Reference** (`src/content/docs/kinswardctl/commands.md`)
- **Current state:** 15 command categories, 80+ individual commands documented
- **Needs update:**
  - New CLI subcommands for game phases (if phase switching is exposed via CLI)
  - New debug/performance commands related to loading screen
  - Updates to `switch-mode` if new modes are added (currently: adventure/overseer/map)
  - Any new query commands for phase information

#### 3. **kinswardctl/Examples** (`src/content/docs/kinswardctl/examples.md`)
- **Needs update:**
  - Practical examples using new v0.26.x features
  - Phase-specific workflows if applicable

#### 4. **Gameplay/Adventure Mode** (`src/content/docs/gameplay/adventure-mode.mdx`)
- **Potential updates:**
  - If loading screen affects Adventure Mode initialization
  - If game phases alter transition mechanics

#### 5. **Gameplay/Overseer Mode** (`src/content/docs/gameplay/overseer-mode.mdx`)
- **Potential updates:**
  - If game phases affect Overseer mode behavior
  - Camera/rendering z-level documentation might relate to loading screen phases

#### 6. **Getting Started/Installation** (`src/content/docs/getting-started/installation.md`)
- **Possible update:**
  - New command-line flags related to debug mode or loading behavior
  - `--debug` flag already documented, but might need clarification

#### 7. **Development/Changelog** (`src/content/docs/development/changelog.md`)
- **Will definitely need:**
  - New v0.26.0 section with game phases, CLI subcommands, debug mode features, loading screen changes
  - Standard format: bold category labels, technical details, system/component names

#### 8. **Index/Landing Page** (`src/content/docs/index.mdx`)
- **Possible update:**
  - If v0.26.0 is significant, update "Current State" section (currently says v0.21.0)
  - Update Bevy version if it changed (currently v0.18)

---

### Key Documentation Patterns to Follow

**For Changelog entries:**
- Use `**Category Name**:` format for bold labels
- Include internal component/system names
- List concrete additions (command names, component names)
- Use semicolons to separate related changes

**For Commands Reference:**
- Use markdown tables with `| Command | Description |` headers
- Include code block examples for each command family
- Group related commands under category headers (`## Section Name`)
- Use inline code for flag names and arguments

**For Examples:**
- Show real-world usage scenarios
- Include command output context ("Lists all companions", "Returns player position")
- Use bash code blocks with comments
- Progress from simple (ping test) to complex (scripting, bulk operations)

**For Controls/Keybindings:**
- Use the `<Kbd>` component from starlight-kbd for platform-specific rendering
- Include note about debug-only controls requiring `--debug` flag
- Organize by context (Global, Adventure Mode, Overseer Mode, etc.)

---

### Architecture & Design Patterns to Reference

The documentation references these key systems for v0.26.x features:

- **InputRegistry** — 21 registered bindings across 5 contexts (already documented in Architecture)
- **GameCommand** — serde-tagged enum for all game commands (routed through CommandQueue)
- **InputMode** — state machine tracking Adventure/Overseer modes
- **SystemSets** — execution order: Input → Commands → Movement → Fov → Lighting → Barriers → Render

These patterns should guide documentation of phase systems and new CLI commands.

---

### Files to Update Summary

| File | Priority | Updates Needed |
|------|----------|---|
| `/src/content/docs/development/changelog.md` | **CRITICAL** | Add v0.26.0 section |
| `/src/content/docs/kinswardctl/commands.md` | **HIGH** | Document new CLI subcommands for phases |
| `/src/content/docs/getting-started/controls.mdx` | **HIGH** | Document F3/F11 debug keys explicitly |
| `/src/content/docs/index.mdx` | **MEDIUM** | Update version from v0.21.0 to v0.26.0 |
| `/src/content/docs/kinswardctl/examples.md` | **MEDIUM** | Add examples using new features |
| `/src/content/docs/getting-started/installation.md` | **MEDIUM** | Clarify debug mode flags if expanded |
| `/src/content/docs/gameplay/adventure-mode.mdx` | **LOW** | Update if phases affect gameplay |
| `/src/content/docs/gameplay/overseer-mode.mdx` | **LOW** | Update if phases affect RTS mechanics |

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Report: Kinsward v0.26.0 and v0.26.1 Changes

Based on my analysis of commits dd96b67e (v0.26.0) and 3fb9b689 (v0.26.1), here are all the detailed changes:

### **v0.26.0: GamePhase State Machine, Loading Screen, CLI Subcommands**

#### **1. GamePhase State Machine**
- **File**: `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/game_phase.rs`
- **Changes**: Expanded `GamePhase` enum from 2 states to 4:
  - `MainMenu` (NEW - now the default state)
  - `WorldGen`
  - `Loading` (NEW)
  - `Playing`
- **Flow**: `MainMenu` → `WorldGen` or `Loading` → `Playing` (depending on CLI command)

#### **2. CLI Subcommands (Restructured)**
- **File**: `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/lib.rs`
- **Old syntax** (removed):
  - `cargo run -- --save quicksave` (load a save)
  - `cargo run -- --seed 42` (create new world with seed)
- **New syntax** (subcommand-based):
  - `cargo run -- new [--seed N]` — Create a new world with optional seed
  - `cargo run -- load <name>` — Load an existing save
  - `cargo run` — Start with main menu (auto-transitions)
  - `cargo run -- --debug new` — Debug mode + new world with random seed
- **Implementation**: 
  - `CliArgs` struct now has `command: Option<CliCommand>` field
  - `CliCommand` enum with `New { seed: Option<u64> }` and `Load { name: String }` variants
  - Added `is_load()` helper method on `CliArgs`

#### **3. Main Menu (New)**
- **File**: `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/main_menu.rs`
- **Features**:
  - Stub screen displaying "KINSWARD" title and "main menu placeholder" text
  - Spawns on `Startup` (after `init_world`)
  - Uses `FocusPolicy::Block` + `ZIndex(300)` to block all interaction
  - Background color: `UiTheme::BG_DEEP`
  - Auto-transitions based on CLI:
    - `load <name>` → `GamePhase::Loading`
    - `new` or no command → `GamePhase::WorldGen`
  - Despawns on exit via `OnExit(GamePhase::MainMenu)` system

#### **4. Loading Screen (New)**
- **File**: `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/loading_screen.rs`
- **Features**:
  - Shown when `load <name>` subcommand is used
  - Displays "KINSWARD" title and "Loading «{save_name}»..." subtitle
  - Uses `FocusPolicy::Block` + `ZIndex(300)` to block all interaction
  - Spawns on `OnEnter(GamePhase::Loading)`
  - Implements loading workflow:
    1. Sends `LoadIntent` message with save name
    2. Waits for `LoadComplete` message
    3. On success: transitions to `GamePhase::Playing`
    4. On failure: exits with error code 1
  - Despawns on `OnExit(GamePhase::Loading)`

#### **5. UI Systems Reorganization**
Moved 12+ UI system spawns from `Startup` to `OnEnter(GamePhase::Playing)`:
- `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/chat/ui.rs` — Chat panel spawn
- `/home/peter-alexeev/Projects/kinsward/src/companions/mod.rs` — Companion panel spawn

- **Observer clicks**: all UI clicks use `On<Pointer<Click>>` observers instead of `Interaction` polling
- **Inventory refactor**: `handle_inventory_interaction` replaced with observer `on_inventory_slot_click` + `handle_inventory_q_drop`
- **Hover via CardBaseColor**: inventory slots and cards use `CardBaseColor` + `Hovered` component instead of manual `PickingInteraction` polling
- **PickingInteraction removed**: completely eliminated from the codebase
- **UiSlotManager**: centralized panel management system
- **DebugPickingPlugin**: behind `cfg(debug)`, toggle with F3; F11 prints HoverMap to stderr
- **bevy_dev_tools**: added under `debug` feature flag

## v0.23.0

- **UI scroll**: mouse-wheel scrolling on all scrollable panels via vendored `bevy_scrollbar`
  - Unit list, companions, creative catalog, crafting, build menu, chat — all scroll with visible scrollbar thumbs
  - Chat: scrollbar visible only when panel open (T key), auto-scroll to bottom on new messages
- **UI picking fix**: removed 5 fullscreen positioning overlays (HotbarUiRoot, BuildMenuOverlay, ToolbarRoot, SettlementBarRoot, OverseerUiRoot) that blocked `bevy_picking` for all UI
- **Hover effects**: `CardBaseColor` component + `Hovered` (CSS-like, includes descendants) for card hover feedback across all panels
- **Build menu redesign**: inventory-style 64×64 grid cards, replaces toolbar when open, 40vh height, grid layout
- **Camera FPS fix**: removed unnecessary `fov_dirty`/`lighting_dirty` flags from overseer camera pan — fixes 180→25 FPS drop during camera movement
- **Sprite/mesh picking disabled**: `require_markers: true` on `SpritePickingSettings`/`MeshPickingSettings` to prevent picking overhead on game world entities
- **Theme constants**: `SCROLLBAR_WIDTH`, `SCROLLBAR_THUMB`, `BORDER_WIDTH` added to `UiTheme`
- **Creative catalog**: proper scroll (only grid scrolls, title fixed), `GlobalZIndex(25)`, width 220px
- **Crafting panel**: scrollbar, `GlobalZIndex(25)`, recipe rebuild cache to prevent per-frame respawn

## v0.22.0

- **Sprite system**: texture atlases, palette, item/unit icons, JetBrains Mono font

## v0.21.0

- **UI redesign**: complete dark theme with floating semi-transparent panels replacing the old embedded UI
- **HUD**: top-left floating overlay with level badge, HP bar, EP bar (no background)
- **InputRegistry**: centralized input binding system with 21 keybindings across 5 contexts (Global, Adventure, Overseer, Inventory, Map View)
- **Control hints**: dynamic context-aware keybinding hints in the bottom-right corner
- **Settlement Bar**: top-center bar showing settlement name, population count, day, and season
- **Overseer Toolbar**: 5-button toolbar (Dig, Build, Pickup, Cancel, Auto) with live task count badges
- **Build Menu**: 8 categories (Walls, Stairs, Stations, Light, Plants, Resources, Water, Terrain) in card layout with inventory counts
- **Unit List panel**: N key toggle, scrollable Colony unit cards with name, race, status, level, HP/EP bars; double-click to possess
- **Unit Detail panel**: right-side inspector with name, level bars, race/trait pills, current task, command queue preview
- **Inventory redesign**: 4x10 grid (40 slots), equipment slot placeholders (Head, Weapon, Body, Shield, Legs, Accessory), stat display (STR, DEX, CON, INT, WIS, CHA), trait pills
- **Craft panel**: station indicator header, ingredient have/need display
- **Admin Menu**: debug-only (`~`) panel with Teleport Home, Spawn Colonist/Enemy, Debug UI toggle, Day/Night, Creative mode, Reveal Map
- **Creative Catalog**: debug+creative mode item browser for spawning items into inventory
- **Pause Menu**: redesigned ESC menu with Continue, Save World, Load (browse saves), Quit
- **Map View**: dedicated map mode (M key) with layer switching (Tab), zoom (Scroll), and pan (WASD)
- **QuickPeek**: hold B for 0.3s+ in Adventure mode to temporarily view Overseer mode with zoom overlay
- **Unit stats**: Health, Energy, and UnitLevel components with per-race base values
- **Panel animations**: slide-in/fade transitions for all floating panels (UiAnimator system)

## v0.20.0

- **Race system**: UnitKind replaced with data-driven Race enum (Dwarf, Goblin, Skeleton, Human, Deer, Wolf) + RaceRegistry from `assets/races.ron`
- **Trait system**: 8 innate traits (Hardy, Keen, Clumsy, etc.) randomly assigned at spawn, managed via kinswardctl
- **Name generation**: compound names from racial syllable tables (`assets/names.ron`) — dwarven, goblin, and human cultures
- **World name**: procedurally generated from World culture table, used as default save name
- **New commands**: QueryTraits, UnitAddTrait, UnitRemoveTrait; race and traits in QueryUnits/QueryUnitInfo
- **SpawnUnit**: accepts `race` + mandatory `faction` instead of `kind`

## v0.19.0

- **Async save**: no frame drops during save — data extracted on main thread, I/O on background thread
- **Entity save/load**: units, player, world items, inventories, and companion state fully persist
- **Quick Save/Load**: F5 to save, F9 to load
- **Save menu**: Escape opens menu with Save World and Load (sub-menu listing all saves with dates)
- **Autosave rotation**: 5 slots (autosave_1 through autosave_5), every 5 minutes
- **Load list fix**: save list now displays correctly on every open
- **Crafting UI fix**: "No recipes available" no longer duplicates every frame

## v0.18.0

- **Save/Load system**: serialization of WorldMap (bincode+zstd per z-level), RegionMap, TaskQueue, entity snapshots
- **Atomic saves**: write to temp dir, rename on completion
- **Crafting system**: 12 recipes, 4 crafting stations (Workbench, Furnace, Sawmill, Crusher)
- **Crafting UI**: C key toggles recipe panel, filtered by inventory and nearby station
- **NearbyStation**: proximity-based station detection (2-tile radius)
- **New items**: ores (Iron, Copper, Gold, Coal, Clay), processed resources (Plank, Stick, Ingots, Brick, Rope, Glass)
- **kinswardctl**: save, load, saves, delete-save, recipes, craft, nearby-station commands
- **Autosave**: every 5 minutes

## v0.17.4

- **Dual game modes**: Adventure (direct control) and Overseer (RTS-style)
- **Procedural world generation**: 16-step climate pipeline with 22 biomes
- **Z-level world**: multiple vertical levels with stairs navigation
- **Block system**: 20+ block types with mining and placement
- **Unit management**: command queues, A* pathfinding, task system
- **Companion system**: follow and station behaviors
- **Visibility**: shadowcasting FOV and dynamic lighting
- **Debug tools**: kinswardctl CLI with 40+ commands, TCP protocol
- **Data-driven**: block and item definitions in RON files
- **Cross-platform**: Linux and Windows builds

---

*Detailed per-version changelogs will be added as tagged releases are published.*

## Two Ways to Play

<CardGrid stagger>
  <Card title="Adventure Mode" icon="rocket">
    Take direct control of a character. Explore the world, mine blocks, place structures, and navigate z-levels in real time with WASD controls.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Overseer Mode" icon="setting">
    Switch to an RTS perspective. Select units, issue move/build/demolish commands, manage task queues, and coordinate your colony from above.

  </Card>

  </Card>

## Current State

Kinsward is in active pre-1.0 development (v0.21.0). Core systems — adventure mode, overseer mode, procedural world generation, unit commands, crafting, the kinswardctl debug tool, and a full dark-themed UI with floating panels — are functional. Advanced AI and textured rendering are planned for future phases.

## Built With

- **Rust** — systems language for performance and safety
- **Bevy 0.18** — ECS game engine
- **Avian2D** — 2D physics
- **kinswardctl** — TCP-based CLI for game control, debugging, and automation

6	## Installing from a Release
7	
8	### Linux
9

13

16	```
17	
18	### Windows
19	
20	Extract the `.zip` archive and run `kinsward.exe`.
21	
22	### Directory Structure
23	
24	The game expects the `assets/` directory to be in the same location as the binary:
25	
26	```
27	kinsward          # or kinsward.exe on Windows
28	assets/
29	├── blocks.ron    # block definitions
30	├── items.ron     # item definitions
31	├── Natural.png   # sprite atlas
32	├── Civilized.png # sprite atlas
33	└── People.png    # sprite atlas
34	```
35	
36	## Command-Line Options
37	
38	| Flag | Description |
39	|------|-------------|
40	| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
41	| `--seed <N>` | Set world generation seed |
42	| `--save <NAME>` | Load a save file on startup, skipping world generation |
43	
44	### Environment Variables
45	
46	| Variable | Description |
47	|----------|-------------|
48	| `DEBUG=true` | Same as `--debug` |
49	| `WORLD_SEED=N` | Fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority) |
50	
51	### Examples

54	# Normal run
55	./kinsward
56	
57	# Debug mode with a specific seed

59	
60	# Load a save file directly (skip world generation)
61	./kinsward --save quicksave
62	
63	# Seed via environment variable
64	WORLD_SEED=42 ./kinsward
65	```

file.content:
---
title: Installation
description: How to install and run Kinsward
---

## Installing from a Release

### Linux

```bash
# Extract the archive
tar xzf kinsward-vX.Y.Z-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

# Run the game
./kinsward
```

### Windows

Extract the `.zip` archive and run `kinsward.exe`.

### Directory Structure

The game expects the `assets/` directory to be in the same location as the binary:

```
kinsward          # or kinsward.exe on Windows
assets/
├── blocks.ron    # block definitions
├── items.ron     # item definitions
├── Natural.png   # sprite atlas
├── Civilized.png # sprite atlas
└── People.png    # sprite atlas
```

## Command-Line Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
| `--seed <N>` | Set world generation seed |
| `--save <NAME>` | Load a save file on startup, skipping world generation |

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `DEBUG=true` | Same as `--debug` |
| `WORLD_SEED=N` | Fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority) |

### Examples

```bash
# Normal run
./kinsward

# Debug mode with a specific seed
./kinsward --debug --seed 42

# Load a save file directly (skip world generation)
./kinsward --save quicksave

# Seed via environment variable
WORLD_SEED=42 ./kinsward
```

content.content:
1	---
2	title: Commands Reference
3	description: Complete kinswardctl command reference
4	---
5	
6	All commands are run as `kinswardctl <command> [args...]`. Add `--json` for raw JSON output.
7	
8	## Meta
9	
10	| Command | Description |
11	|---------|-------------|
12	| `ping` | Test connection |
13	| `info` | Server info (version, uptime) |
14	| `game-info` | Extended game info (seed, player position, loaded chunks) |
15	| `perf` | Per-system timing breakdown |
16	
17	```bash
18	kinswardctl ping
19	kinswardctl info
20	kinswardctl game-info
21	kinswardctl perf
22	```
23	
24	## Player State
25	
26	| Command | Description |
27	|---------|-------------|
28	| `state` | Player position, z-level, inventory, hotbar |
29	| `blocked` | All blocked movement directions |
30	| `can-move <dir>` | Check if movement in a direction is possible |
31	
32	```bash
33	kinswardctl state
34	kinswardctl blocked
35	kinswardctl can-move north
36	```
37	
38	:::tip
39	Use `blocked` instead of multiple `can-move` calls — it returns all blocked directions at once.
40	:::
41	
42	## Movement
43	
44	| Command | Description |
45	|---------|-------------|
46	| `move <dir>` | Move in a cardinal direction (north/south/east/west) |
47	| `move-delta <dx> <dy>` | Move by tile offset |
48	| `stairs <dir>` | Use stairs (up/down) |
49	| `teleport <x> <y> <z>` | Teleport to coordinates |
50	
51	```bash
52	kinswardctl move north
53	kinswardctl move-delta 3 0
54	kinswardctl stairs up
55	kinswardctl teleport 10 5 2
56	```
57	
58	## World Queries
59	
60	| Command | Description |
61	|---------|-------------|
62	| `block <x> <y> <z>` | Block info at coordinates |
63	| `surfaces <x> <y>` | Walkable z-levels at (x, y) |
64	| `layer -z <z> -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max>` | Top-down XY view of a z-level |
65	| `cross-section -y <y> -x <min>:<max> -z <min>:<max>` | Side XZ view |
66	| `volume -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <min>:<max>` | 3D volume snapshot |
67	| `nearby <radius>` | Items near the player |
68	| `fov <radius>` | Field of view visibility map |
69	
70	```bash
71	kinswardctl block 5 5 1
72	kinswardctl surfaces 0 0
73	kinswardctl layer -z 1 -x -5:5 -y -5:5
74	kinswardctl cross-section -y 0 -x -10:10 -z -2:3
75	kinswardctl volume -x -3:3 -y -3:3 -z 0:2
76	kinswardctl nearby 5
77	kinswardctl fov 10
78	```
79	
80	## World Modification
81	
82	| Command | Description |
83	|---------|-------------|
84	| `mine-block <x> <y> <z>` | Remove a block |
85	| `place-block <x> <y> <z> <item_id>` | Place a block from inventory |
86	| `set-block <x> <y> <z> <block_type>` | Set block type directly |
87	| `fill -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <z> <block_type>` | Fill area with a block type |
88	| `rebuild-surfaces` | Rebuild the surface index |
89	
90	```bash
91	kinswardctl mine-block 5 5 1
92	kinswardctl place-block 5 5 1 100
93	kinswardctl set-block 5 5 1 40
94	kinswardctl fill -x -5:5 -y -5:5 -z 1 40
95	kinswardctl rebuild-surfaces
96	```
97	
98	## Inventory
99	
100	| Command | Description |
101	|---------|-------------|
102	| `pickup-nearest` | Pick up the nearest item |
103	| `drop-from-slot <slot> [count]` | Drop items from a slot |
104	| `select-hotbar <slot>` | Select a hotbar slot (0-9) |
105	| `inventory` | Full inventory of the focused entity |
106	
107	```bash
108	kinswardctl pickup-nearest
109	kinswardctl drop-from-slot 0 5
110	kinswardctl select-hotbar 3
111	kinswardctl inventory
112	```
113	
114	## Registry
115	
116	| Command | Description |
117	|---------|-------------|
118	| `registry <kind>` | List all entries (blocks / items / floor-covers) |
119	| `lookup <kind> <query>` | Search by name or ID |
120	
121	```bash
122	kinswardctl registry blocks
123	kinswardctl registry items
124	kinswardctl lookup blocks stone
125	kinswardctl lookup items 100
126	```
127	
128	## Units
129	
130	| Command | Description |
131	|---------|-------------|
132	| `units` | List all units |
133	| `unit-info <entity_id>` | Detailed unit info |
134	| `unit-move <entity_id> <x> <y> <z>` | Queue move command |
135	| `unit-build <entity_id> <x> <y> <z> <item_id>` | Queue build command |
136	| `unit-demolish <entity_id> <x> <y> <z>` | Queue demolish command |
137	| `unit-pickup <entity_id> <target_entity_id>` | Queue pickup command |
138	| `unit-plan <entity_id> <waypoints...>` | Multi-waypoint route (x,y,z format) |
139	| `unit-queue-clear <entity_id>` | Clear command queue |
140	| `unit-stop <entity_id>` | Immediate stop |
141	| `unit-cancel-task <entity_id> [--tile x,y,z] [--index N]` | Cancel specific task |
142	| `unit-follow <follower_id> <target_id>` | Make unit follow another |
143	
144	```bash
145	kinswardctl units
146	kinswardctl unit-info 4294967305
147	kinswardctl unit-move 4294967305 10 5 1
148	kinswardctl unit-build 4294967305 10 5 1 100
149	kinswardctl unit-demolish 4294967305 10 5 1
150	kinswardctl unit-plan 4294967305 5,0,1 5,5,1 0,5,1
151	kinswardctl unit-queue-clear 4294967305
152	kinswardctl unit-follow 4294967305 4294967306
153	```
154	
155	## Traits
156	
157	| Command | Description |
158	|---------|-------------|
159	| `traits` | List all available traits |
160	| `unit-add-trait <entity_id> <trait_id>` | Add a trait to a unit |
161	| `unit-remove-trait <entity_id> <trait_id>` | Remove a trait from a unit |
162	
163	Trait IDs: `hardy`, `quicklearner`, `clumsy`, `nightowl`, `strongback`, `keen`, `frail`, `firstborn`.
164	
165	```bash
166	kinswardctl traits
167	kinswardctl unit-add-trait 4294967305 hardy
168	kinswardctl unit-remove-trait 4294967305 clumsy
169	```
170	
171	## Spawning
172	
173	| Command | Description |
174	|---------|-------------|
175	| `spawn <race> <x> <y> <z> [options]` | Spawn a unit |
176	| `possess [entity_id]` | Possess a unit (no ID = release) |
177

---
title: Commands Reference
description: Complete kinswardctl command reference
---

All commands are run as `kinswardctl <command> [args...]`. Add `--json` for raw JSON output.

## Meta

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `ping` | Test connection |
| `info` | Server info (version, uptime) |
| `game-info` | Extended game info (seed, player position, loaded chunks) |
| `perf` | Per-system timing breakdown |

```bash
kinswardctl ping
kinswardctl info
kinswardctl game-info
kinswardctl perf
```

## Player State

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `state` | Player position, z-level, inventory, hotbar |
| `blocked` | All blocked movement directions |
| `can-move <dir>` | Check if movement in a direction is possible |

```bash
kinswardctl state
kinswardctl blocked
kinswardctl can-move north
```

:::tip
Use `blocked` instead of multiple `can-move` calls — it returns all blocked directions at once.
:::

## Movement

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `move <dir>` | Move in a cardinal direction (north/south/east/west) |
| `move-delta <dx> <dy>` | Move by tile offset |
| `stairs <dir>` | Use stairs (up/down) |
| `teleport <x> <y> <z>` | Teleport to coordinates |

```bash
kinswardctl move north
kinswardctl move-delta 3 0
kinswardctl stairs up
kinswardctl teleport 10 5 2
```

## World Queries

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `block <x> <y> <z>` | Block info at coordinates |
| `surfaces <x> <y>` | Walkable z-levels at (x, y) |
| `layer -z <z> -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max>` | Top-down XY view of a z-level |
| `cross-section -y <y> -x <min>:<max> -z <min>:<max>` | Side XZ view |
| `volume -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <min>:<max>` | 3D volume snapshot |
| `nearby <radius>` | Items near the player |
| `fov <radius>` | Field of view visibility map |

```bash
kinswardctl block 5 5 1
kinswardctl surfaces 0 0
kinswardctl layer -z 1 -x -5:5 -y -5:5
kinswardctl cross-section -y 0 -x -10:10 -z -2:3
kinswardctl volume -x -3:3 -y -3:3 -z 0:2
kinswardctl nearby 5
kinswardctl fov 10
```

## World Modification

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `mine-block <x> <y> <z>` | Remove a block |
| `place-block <x> <y> <z> <item_id>` | Place a block from inventory |
| `set-block <x> <y> <z> <block_type>` | Set block type directly |
| `fill -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <z> <block_type>` | Fill area with a block type |
| `rebuild-surfaces` | Rebuild the surface index |

```bash
kinswardctl mine-block 5 5 1
kinswardctl place-block 5 5 1 100
kinswardctl set-block 5 5 1 40
kinswardctl fill -x -5:5 -y -5:5 -z 1 40
kinswardctl rebuild-surfaces
```

## Inventory

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `pickup-nearest` | Pick up the nearest item |
| `drop-from-slot <slot> [count]` | Drop items from a slot |
| `select-hotbar <slot>` | Select a hotbar slot (0-9) |
| `inventory` | Full inventory of the focused entity |

```bash
kinswardctl pickup-nearest
kinswardctl drop-from-slot 0 5
kinswardctl select-hotbar 3
kinswardctl inventory
```

## Registry

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `registry <kind>` | List all entries (blocks / items / floor-covers) |
| `lookup <kind> <query>` | Search by name or ID |

```bash
kinswardctl registry blocks
kinswardctl registry items
kinswardctl lookup blocks stone
kinswardctl lookup items 100
```

## Units

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `units` | List all units |
| `unit-info <entity_id>` | Detailed unit info |
| `unit-move <entity_id> <x> <y> <z>` | Queue move command |
| `unit-build <entity_id> <x> <y> <z> <item_id>` | Queue build command |
| `unit-demolish <entity_id> <x> <y> <z>` | Queue demolish command |
| `unit-pickup <entity_id> <target_entity_id>` | Queue pickup command |
| `unit-plan <entity_id> <waypoints...>` | Multi-waypoint route (x,y,z format) |
| `unit-queue-clear <entity_id>` | Clear command queue |
| `unit-stop <entity_id>` | Immediate stop |
| `unit-cancel-task <entity_id> [--tile x,y,z] [--index N]` | Cancel specific task |
| `unit-follow <follower_id> <target_id>` | Make unit follow another |

```bash
kinswardctl units
kinswardctl unit-info 4294967305
kinswardctl unit-move 4294967305 10 5 1
kinswardctl unit-build 4294967305 10 5 1 100
kinswardctl unit-demolish 4294967305 10 5 1
kinswardctl unit-plan 4294967305 5,0,1 5,5,1 0,5,1
kinswardctl unit-queue-clear 4294967305
kinswardctl unit-follow 4294967305 4294967306
```

## Traits

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `traits` | List all available traits |
| `unit-add-trait <entity_id> <trait_id>` | Add a trait to a unit |
| `unit-remove-trait <entity_id> <trait_id>` | Remove a trait from a unit |

Trait IDs: `hardy`, `quicklearner`, `clumsy`, `nightowl`, `strongback`, `keen`, `frail`, `firstborn`.

```bash
kinswardctl traits
kinswardctl unit-add-trait 4294967305 hardy
kinswardctl unit-remove-trait 4294967305 clumsy
```

## Spawning

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `spawn <race> <x> <y> <z> [options]` | Spawn a unit |
| `possess [entity_id]` | Possess a unit (no ID = release) |

Races: `dwarf`, `goblin`, `skeleton`, `human`, `deer`, `wolf`.

Options for `spawn`: `--name <name>`, `--faction <faction>` (default: colony), `--controlled`.
Factions: `colony`, `hostile`, `neutral`, `wild`.

```bash
kinswardctl spawn dwarf 10 5 1 --name "Urist" --faction colony --controlled
kinswardctl spawn goblin 5 5 1 --faction hostile
kinswardctl possess 4294967305
kinswardctl possess
```

## Zone Operations

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `zone-demolish --units <ids> -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <z> [--append]` | Bulk demolish |
| `zone-build --units <ids> -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <z> --item <id> [--append]` | Bulk build |
| `zone-cancel [--units <ids>] -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <z>` | Cancel zone tasks |

```bash
kinswardctl zone-demolish --units 4294967305,4294967306 -x -5:5 -y -5:5 -z 1
kinswardctl zone-build --units 4294967305 -x 0:10 -y 0:10 -z 1 --item 100
kinswardctl zone-cancel -x -5:5 -y -5:5 -z 1
```

Use `--append` to add to existing task queues instead of replacing them.

## Companions

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `companion-list` | List all companions |
| `companion-behavior <entity_id> <behavior>` | Set behavior (follow/station) |
| `companion-call-all` | Set all companions to Follow |
| `companion-dismiss-all` | Set all companions to Station |

```bash
kinswardctl companion-list
kinswardctl companion-behavior 4294967305 follow
kinswardctl companion-call-all
kinswardctl companion-dismiss-all
```

## Tasks

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `tasks [--status <filter>]` | List tasks (all/pending/assigned/blocked) |
| `task-add <kind> <x> <y> <z> [--item <id>] [--target <id>]` | Create a task |
| `task-cancel <task_id>` | Cancel a task |
| `task-cancel-zone -x <min>:<max> -y <min>:<max> -z <z>` | Cancel all tasks in zone |

```bash
kinswardctl tasks
kinswardctl tasks --status pending
kinswardctl task-add build 10 5 1 --item 100
kinswardctl task-add demolish 10 5 1
kinswardctl task-cancel 42
kinswardctl task-cancel-zone -x -5:5 -y -5:5 -z 1
```

## Region Map

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `region-map <layer> [options]` | Export region map layer |

Layers: `elevation`, `temperature`, `rainfall`, `wind`, `drainage`, `rivers`, `geology`, `fertility`, `vegetation`, `plates`, `biome`.

Options: `--format <png/ascii/stats>`, `--path <output>`, `--size <N>`, `--width <N>`, `-x <N>`, `-y <N>`.

```bash
kinswardctl region-map elevation
kinswardctl region-map elevation --format png --path elevation.png --size 1024
kinswardctl region-map rivers --format ascii
kinswardctl region-map biome --format stats
```

## Crafting

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `recipes [--station <name>] [--all]` | List crafting recipes (default: available only) |
| `nearby-station` | Check which crafting station is nearby |
| `craft <recipe_id>` | Craft a recipe by ID |

```bash
kinswardctl recipes                          # available recipes
kinswardctl recipes --all                    # all recipes
kinswardctl recipes --station FURNACE --all  # recipes for a station
kinswardctl nearby-station
kinswardctl craft 1
```

## Save & Load

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `save [name]` | Save the world (default: `world_{seed}`) |
| `load <name>` | Load a saved world |
| `saves` | List all save slots |
| `delete-save <name>` | Delete a save slot |

```bash
kinswardctl save
kinswardctl save my_world
kinswardctl saves
kinswardctl load quicksave
kinswardctl delete-save old_save
```

## Mode & View

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `switch-mode <mode>` | Switch game mode (adventure / overseer / map) |
| `set-tool-mode <mode>` | Set overseer tool mode (auto / force-build / force-demolish / force-pickup / force-cancel) |
| `set-active-z <z>` | Set the active z-level for rendering |

```bash
kinswardctl switch-mode overseer
kinswardctl switch-mode adventure
kinswardctl set-tool-mode auto
kinswardctl set-active-z 0
```

## Camera

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `camera-move <x> <y>` | Move camera by tile offset (overseer mode) |
| `camera-zoom <level>` | Set camera zoom level (0.1–20.0) |

```bash
kinswardctl camera-move 5 5
kinswardctl camera-move -10 0
kinswardctl camera-zoom 2.0
```

## Selection

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `select <entity_id>` | Select a unit |
| `deselect-all` | Deselect all units |
| `selection` | Query current selection and focus |

```bash
kinswardctl select 4294967305
kinswardctl selection
kinswardctl deselect-all
```

## Floor Covers

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `set-floor-cover <x> <y> <z> <cover>` | Set a floor cover at coordinates |
| `remove-floor-cover <x> <y> <z>` | Remove a floor cover |

Cover names: `carpet`, `blood_stain`, `puddle`.

```bash
kinswardctl set-floor-cover 5 5 0 carpet
kinswardctl remove-floor-cover 5 5 0
```

## Lighting

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `light <x> <y> <z>` | Query light value at coordinates (0.0–1.0) |

```bash
kinswardctl light 0 0 1
kinswardctl light 5 5 0
```

## Raw Command

Send arbitrary JSON directly to the TCP server:

```bash
kinswardctl raw '{"cmd":"Ping"}'
kinswardctl raw '{"cmd":"QueryState"}'
```

Документация сайта покрывает до v0.25.0. В игре вышли v0.26.0 (GamePhase стейт-машина, CLI subcommands, экраны загрузки/главного меню) и v0.26.1 (debug-функции только с --debug). Нужно отразить эти изменения.

### 1. `src/content/docs/development/changelog.md`
- Заменить `v0.25.0 (Current)` → `v0.25.0`

- Добавить F3 (toggle debug picking: Off → Picking → Hitting) и F11 (hover debug info) в таблицу Debug

- Обновить версию в "Current State" с v0.21.0 на v0.26.1
- Обновить описание: упомянуть game phases и CLI subcommands

## Verification

```bash

```

---
title: Changelog
description: Kinsward release history
---

Release notes are also available on the [GitHub Releases page](https://github.com/alexeev-dauwalter/kinsward-site/releases).

## v0.25.0 (Current)

- **14 new GameCommand variants**: SwitchMode, SetToolMode, CameraMove, CameraZoom, SelectUnit, DeselectAll, QuerySelection, SetActiveZ, SetFloorCover, RemoveFloorCover, QueryLight, QueryInventory, QueryPerf
- **Command stubs connected**: SaveWorld/LoadWorld wired to SaveIntent/LoadIntent; QueryRecipes/Craft/QueryNearbyStation wired to CraftingRegistry
- **SystemParam bundles**: `SpawnCmd` and `ExtCmd` bundles to work around Bevy's 16-param limit
- **CameraCmdQueue**: dedicated queue + `apply_camera_commands` system for camera commands from TCP
- **kinswardctl**: 12 new subcommands with human-readable formatting (switch-mode, set-tool-mode, camera-move, camera-zoom, select, deselect-all, selection, set-active-z, set-floor-cover, remove-floor-cover, light, perf)

## v0.24.0

- **UI picking migration**: `UiHovered` resource (centralized hover check from `HoverMap` filtered by `Node`) replaces 6 `Query<&PickingInteraction>` guard patterns
- **Observer clicks**: all UI clicks use `On<Pointer<Click>>` observers instead of `Interaction` polling
- **Inventory refactor**: `handle_inventory_interaction` replaced with observer `on_inventory_slot_click` + `handle_inventory_q_drop`
- **Hover via CardBaseColor**: inventory slots and cards use `CardBaseColor` + `Hovered` component instead of manual `PickingInteraction` polling
- **PickingInteraction removed**: completely eliminated from the codebase
- **UiSlotManager**: centralized panel management system
- **DebugPickingPlugin**: behind `cfg(debug)`, toggle with F3; F11 prints HoverMap to stderr
- **bevy_dev_tools**: added under `debug` feature flag

## v0.23.0

- **UI scroll**: mouse-wheel scrolling on all scrollable panels via vendored `bevy_scrollbar`
  - Unit list, companions, creative catalog, crafting, build menu, chat — all scroll with visible scrollbar thumbs
  - Chat: scrollbar visible only when panel open (T key), auto-scroll to bottom on new messages
- **UI picking fix**: removed 5 fullscreen positioning overlays (HotbarUiRoot, BuildMenuOverlay, ToolbarRoot, SettlementBarRoot, OverseerUiRoot) that blocked `bevy_picking` for all UI
- **Hover effects**: `CardBaseColor` component + `Hovered` (CSS-like, includes descendants) for card hover feedback across all panels
- **Build menu redesign**: inventory-style 64×64 grid cards, replaces toolbar when open, 40vh height, grid layout
- **Camera FPS fix**: removed unnecessary `fov_dirty`/`lighting_dirty` flags from overseer camera pan — fixes 180→25 FPS drop during camera movement
- **Sprite/mesh picking disabled**: `require_markers: true` on `SpritePickingSettings`/`MeshPickingSettings` to prevent picking overhead on game world entities
- **Theme constants**: `SCROLLBAR_WIDTH`, `SCROLLBAR_THUMB`, `BORDER_WIDTH` added to `UiTheme`
- **Creative catalog**: proper scroll (only grid scrolls, title fixed), `GlobalZIndex(25)`, width 220px
- **Crafting panel**: scrollbar, `GlobalZIndex(25)`, recipe rebuild cache to prevent per-frame respawn

## v0.22.0

- **Sprite system**: texture atlases, palette, item/unit icons, JetBrains Mono font

## v0.21.0

- **UI redesign**: complete dark theme with floating semi-transparent panels replacing the old embedded UI
- **HUD**: top-left floating overlay with level badge, HP bar, EP bar (no background)
- **InputRegistry**: centralized input binding system with 21 keybindings across 5 contexts (Global, Adventure, Overseer, Inventory, Map View)
- **Control hints**: dynamic context-aware keybinding hints in the bottom-right corner
- **Settlement Bar**: top-center bar showing settlement name, population count, day, and season
- **Overseer Toolbar**: 5-button toolbar (Dig, Build, Pickup, Cancel, Auto) with live task count badges
- **Build Menu**: 8 categories (Walls, Stairs, Stations, Light, Plants, Resources, Water, Terrain) in card layout with inventory counts
- **Unit List panel**: N key toggle, scrollable Colony unit cards with name, race, status, level, HP/EP bars; double-click to possess
- **Unit Detail panel**: right-side inspector with name, level bars, race/trait pills, current task, command queue preview
- **Inventory redesign**: 4x10 grid (40 slots), equipment slot placeholders (Head, Weapon, Body, Shield, Legs, Accessory), stat display (STR, DEX, CON, INT, WIS, CHA), trait pills
- **Craft panel**: station indicator header, ingredient have/need display
- **Admin Menu**: debug-only (`~`) panel with Teleport Home, Spawn Colonist/Enemy, Debug UI toggle, Day/Night, Creative mode, Reveal Map
- **Creative Catalog**: debug+creative mode item browser for spawning items into inventory
- **Pause Menu**: redesigned ESC menu with Continue, Save World, Load (browse saves), Quit
- **Map View**: dedicated map mode (M key) with layer switching (Tab), zoom (Scroll), and pan (WASD)
- **QuickPeek**: hold B for 0.3s+ in Adventure mode to temporarily view Overseer mode with zoom overlay
- **Unit stats**: Health, Energy, and UnitLevel components with per-race base values
- **Panel animations**: slide-in/fade transitions for all floating panels (UiAnimator system)

## v0.20.0

- **Race system**: UnitKind replaced with data-driven Race enum (Dwarf, Goblin, Skeleton, Human, Deer, Wolf) + RaceRegistry from `assets/races.ron`
- **Trait system**: 8 innate traits (Hardy, Keen, Clumsy, etc.) randomly assigned at spawn, managed via kinswardctl
- **Name generation**: compound names from racial syllable tables (`assets/names.ron`) — dwarven, goblin, and human cultures
- **World name**: procedurally generated from World culture table, used as default save name
- **New commands**: QueryTraits, UnitAddTrait, UnitRemoveTrait; race and traits in QueryUnits/QueryUnitInfo
- **SpawnUnit**: accepts `race` + mandatory `faction` instead of `kind`

## v0.19.0

- **Async save**: no frame drops during save — data extracted on main thread, I/O on background thread
- **Entity save/load**: units, player, world items, inventories, and companion state fully persist
- **Quick Save/Load**: F5 to save, F9 to load
- **Save menu**: Escape opens menu with Save World and Load (sub-menu listing all saves with dates)
- **Autosave rotation**: 5 slots (autosave_1 through autosave_5), every 5 minutes
- **Load list fix**: save list now displays correctly on every open
- **Crafting UI fix**: "No recipes available" no longer duplicates every frame

## v0.18.0

- **Save/Load system**: serialization of WorldMap (bincode+zstd per z-level), RegionMap, TaskQueue, entity snapshots
- **Atomic saves**: write to temp dir, rename on completion
- **Crafting system**: 12 recipes, 4 crafting stations (Workbench, Furnace, Sawmill, Crusher)
- **Crafting UI**: C key toggles recipe panel, filtered by inventory and nearby station
- **NearbyStation**: proximity-based station detection (2-tile radius)
- **New items**: ores (Iron, Copper, Gold, Coal, Clay), processed resources (Plank, Stick, Ingots, Brick, Rope, Glass)
- **kinswardctl**: save, load, saves, delete-save, recipes, craft, nearby-station commands
- **Autosave**: every 5 minutes

## v0.17.4

- **Dual game modes**: Adventure (direct control) and Overseer (RTS-style)
- **Procedural world generation**: 16-step climate pipeline with 22 biomes
- **Z-level world**: multiple vertical levels with stairs navigation
- **Block system**: 20+ block types with mining and placement
- **Unit management**: command queues, A* pathfinding, task system
- **Companion system**: follow and station behaviors
- **Visibility**: shadowcasting FOV and dynamic lighting
- **Debug tools**: kinswardctl CLI with 40+ commands, TCP protocol
- **Data-driven**: block and item definitions in RON files
- **Cross-platform**: Linux and Windows builds

## Command-Line Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
| `--seed <N>` | Set world generation seed |
| `--save <NAME>` | Load a save file on startup, skipping world generation |

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `DEBUG=true` | Same as `--debug` |
| `WORLD_SEED=N` | Fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority) |

### Examples

```bash
# Normal run
./kinsward

# Debug mode with a specific seed
./kinsward --debug --seed 42

# Load a save file directly (skip world generation)
./kinsward --save quicksave

# Seed via environment variable
WORLD_SEED=42 ./kinsward
```

---
title: Installation
description: How to install and run Kinsward
---

## Installing from a Release

### Linux

```bash
# Extract the archive
tar xzf kinsward-vX.Y.Z-linux-x86_64.tar.gz

# Run the game
./kinsward
```

### Windows

Extract the `.zip` archive and run `kinsward.exe`.

### Directory Structure

The game expects the `assets/` directory to be in the same location as the binary:

```
kinsward          # or kinsward.exe on Windows
assets/
├── blocks.ron    # block definitions
├── items.ron     # item definitions
├── Natural.png   # sprite atlas
├── Civilized.png # sprite atlas
└── People.png    # sprite atlas
```

## Command-Line Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--debug` | Enable debug mode (physics gizmos, test items, admin panel) |
| `--seed <N>` | Set world generation seed |
| `--save <NAME>` | Load a save file on startup, skipping world generation |

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `DEBUG=true` | Same as `--debug` |
| `WORLD_SEED=N` | Fallback seed (CLI `--seed` takes priority) |

### Examples

```bash
# Normal run
./kinsward

# Debug mode with a specific seed
./kinsward --debug --seed 42

# Load a save file directly (skip world generation)
./kinsward --save quicksave

# Seed via environment variable
WORLD_SEED=42 ./kinsward
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
title: Kinsward
description: A Dwarf Fortress-inspired colony sim with adventure and overseer modes
template: splash
hero:
  tagline: A 2D colony sim with z-levels, dual game modes, and deep procedural world generation. Explore, build, and command — all in the same world.
  actions:
    - text: Download Latest
      link: https://github.com/alexeev-dauwalter/kinsward-site/releases/latest
      icon: down-caret
      attrs:
        target: _blank
    - text: Read the Docs
      link: /getting-started/download/
      icon: right-arrow
      variant: minimal
---

import { Card, CardGrid } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

## Two Ways to Play

<CardGrid stagger>
  <Card title="Adventure Mode" icon="rocket">
    Take direct control of a character. Explore the world, mine blocks, place structures, and navigate z-levels in real time with WASD controls.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Overseer Mode" icon="setting">
    Switch to an RTS perspective. Select units, issue move/build/demolish commands, manage task queues, and coordinate your colony from above.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Living World" icon="sun">
    Procedurally generated worlds with a 16-step climate pipeline — tectonic plates, rainfall simulation, river erosion, 22 biomes, and realistic vegetation.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Deep Systems" icon="puzzle">
    Z-levels with stairs, shadowcasting FOV, dynamic lighting, A* pathfinding, companion AI, and a full TCP debug protocol for scripting and automation.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

## Current State

Kinsward is in active pre-1.0 development (v0.21.0). Core systems — adventure mode, overseer mode, procedural world generation, unit commands, crafting, the kinswardctl debug tool, and a full dark-themed UI with floating panels — are functional. Advanced AI and textured rendering are planned for future phases.

## Built With

- **Rust** — systems language for performance and safety
- **Bevy 0.18** — ECS game engine
- **Avian2D** — 2D physics
- **kinswardctl** — TCP-based CLI for game control, debugging, and automation

Advanced AI and textured rendering are planned for future phases.
